jokes.text: Lending? Makes it sound like I had a choice in the matter. I never should've agreed to this or let you know that I have a cabin or gotten to know any of you.
jokes.setup: Leslie thanks Ron for 'lending' his cabin
jokes.punchline: Ron's escalating regret - from agreeing to the cabin, to revealing he has one, to meeting anyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - his antisocial nature escalating to existential regret about human connection

jokes.text: I don't wanna over-hype it, but our parade makes the rose bowl parade look like a turds-on-wheels convention.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing Pawnee's founder's week parade with typical enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous comparison to 'turds-on-wheels convention'
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual imagery with Leslie's characteristic over-the-top civic pride

jokes.text: Easy: Most murders per capita. The guillotine was invented here. City Hall is run by the walrus mafia.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April what makes Pawnee the best city
jokes.punchline: April's characteristically dark and absurd responses
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - dark humor delivered deadpan, 'walrus mafia' is particularly absurd

jokes.text: Cost of living is cheap. In about three years, I will have saved enough to pay off my condo in Seattle.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Donna what makes Pawnee great
jokes.punchline: Donna's practical response reveals she's planning to leave
jokes.explanation: Classic Donna pragmatism - completely missing Leslie's point while revealing her escape plan

jokes.text: You have a condo in Seattle? / Yes, I like the rain and the fish markets.
jokes.setup: Leslie's surprise at Donna's revelation
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact explanation of her secret life preferences
jokes.explanation: Reveals Donna's hidden depths in characteristic understated way

jokes.text: I'm bored. Let's go have sex in a tree.
jokes.setup: Meeting about founder's week planning
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa's completely inappropriate and bizarre suggestion
jokes.explanation: Peak Mona-Lisa awfulness - inappropriate timing and bizarre location choice

jokes.text: He'll be back in eight minutes.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa drags Tom away for tree sex
jokes.punchline: April's precise timing estimate delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with the specificity of 'eight minutes' making it funnier than just 'soon'

jokes.text: I don't really have any power in this relationship.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Tom brought Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Tom's admission of complete powerlessness
jokes.explanation: Tom's pathetic admission plays into his character's lack of backbone with women

jokes.text: Side note: I accidentally threw my sweat shirt away. That's why I was digging through the trash. Found that too. Pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why he was digging through Ron's trash
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact delivery about accidentally throwing away his own shirt
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy logic - accidentally throwing away his own shirt and being pleased to find it again

jokes.text: They said I was retired. They said I was too dangerous for the Pawnee Police Department. Turns out they were right... and wrong. Macklin, you son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Andy decides to investigate the pregnancy test as Bert Macklin
jokes.punchline: His dramatic internal monologue ending with addressing himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Bert Macklin moment - the self-address at the end is peak Andy absurdity

jokes.text: Due to a tragic misunderstanding, the prettiest pig beauty pageant has been replaced by a pork rib barbecue competition.
jokes.setup: Announcement about founder's week changes
jokes.punchline: The darkly comic implication about what happened to the pageant pigs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the euphemistic 'tragic misunderstanding' makes the pig fate even funnier

jokes.text: And frankly, I don't see any of those citizens standing up and complaining abo-- Okay. Now, I see them.
jokes.setup: Leslie arguing that citizens aren't complaining
jokes.punchline: Immediately being proven wrong mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy timing with Leslie's confidence immediately undermined by reality.

jokes.text: Whoa! Darren! Slow down! That sweat suit's not for sweatin' in, okay? This is crushed velvet. If you go at more than a brisk walk, it will fall apart.
jokes.setup: Customer trying on rental clothes at Tom's store
jokes.punchline: The absurd impracticality of athletic wear that can't handle exercise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom business logic - style over any practical function

jokes.text: I could add a baby section over there, call it 'Li'l Swaggers.' I could add some old people stuff, call it 'Rent-A-Sag.'
jokes.setup: Tom brainstorming business expansion
jokes.punchline: The terrible puns, especially 'Rent-A-Sag' for elderly clothes
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom business names - the progression from cute to offensive is character-perfect

jokes.text: Ugh, no. Old people are gross.
jokes.setup: Tom's business expansion idea for elderly customers
jokes.punchline: His immediate dismissal based on shallow prejudice
jokes.explanation: Very in-character for Tom but fairly predictable shallow response

jokes.text: He sounded really sexy on the phone, and I was very into that. Like, I was, like, very into that.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa telling Tom about a lawyer who called
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate oversharing about attraction to a stranger's voice
jokes.explanation: Peak Mona-Lisa inappropriateness but the repetition makes it feel forced

jokes.text: Okay. I frenched my cousin one time. / That's gross. / I might still be in love with him.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to set boundaries with Mona-Lisa
jokes.punchline: Her immediate violation with increasingly disturbing overshare
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of awfulness - just as Tom sets boundaries, she violates them spectacularly

jokes.text: Babyface heard my demo? I will accept a record deal.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions a client interested in Tom's business
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately assumes it's about his music career
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom delusion - assumes any business interest must be about his failed music career

jokes.text: Whoa, Diddy wants to buy Rent-A-Swag? / Not what I said. / This is a lot to process. Will Diddy be coming to the meeting?
jokes.setup: Lawyer explains confidential client interest
jokes.punchline: Tom's obsession with it being Diddy despite clear denials
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag of Tom's Diddy obsession - his inability to hear 'no' is very in character

jokes.text: Oh, boy. What do you want, Macklin? / Me? Just observing. A little something they taught us down at Quantico.
jokes.setup: Andy approaches Ann in his Bert Macklin persona
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous claim about FBI training at Quantico
jokes.explanation: Good Bert Macklin moment - Ann's weary recognition adds to the comedy

jokes.text: Congratulations. / Is that a question? / It's an accusation, 'cause I know your secret. You're pregnant.
jokes.setup: Andy's strange approach to questioning Ann
jokes.punchline: His dramatic 'accusation' reveal about pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Good detective parody structure - treating congratulations as an accusation is absurd

jokes.text: I'm trying to get pregnant. That's not fair. I mean, great, and happy for them, and congratulations, whatever, but ooh! No! Boo!
jokes.setup: Ann learns someone else might be pregnant
jokes.punchline: Her attempt to be gracious falling apart into petty jealousy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ann's attempt at maturity crumbling into honest emotion

jokes.text: Whoa, I don't think so. Bert Macklin works alone. I will buy lunch. You can have as many burgers as you want. Welcome to the FBI.
jokes.setup: Andy refusing partnership
jokes.punchline: Immediately accepting when offered free food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - principled detective stance immediately abandoned for food.

jokes.text: Whoa, I don't think so. Bert Macklin works alone. / I will buy lunch. You can have as many burgers as you want. / Welcome to the FBI.
jokes.setup: Andy refusing partnership in his investigation
jokes.punchline: His immediate capitulation when offered unlimited burgers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his principles immediately abandoned for food, great comedic structure

jokes.text: Pawnee is no longer the fourth most obese city in America. We're now ninth.
jokes.setup: Ben defending Leslie's health initiatives
jokes.punchline: The 'improvement' still being embarrassingly bad
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about municipal 'progress' - still being terrible but less so

jokes.text: You somehow convinced the school board that napkins are a vegetable. / They're made from plants.
jokes.setup: Criticism of unhealthy school lunches
jokes.punchline: The absurd justification for napkins as vegetables
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - technically true but completely missing the point, highly quotable

jokes.text: Ever since you stripped us of our freedoms by making us hire women, we've had to turn one of our bathrooms into a woman's bathroom. We only have one bathroom now.
jokes.setup: Complaint about Leslie's policies
jokes.punchline: Framing basic equality as oppression, revealing the absurd previous situation
jokes.explanation: Good satire of resistance to basic workplace equality

jokes.text: I'm so sorry, Dewey. That sounds like a horrible price to pay for something as trivial as gender equality.
jokes.setup: Dewey's complaint about bathroom equality
jokes.punchline: Leslie's perfectly sarcastic response calling gender equality 'trivial'
jokes.explanation: Perfect sarcastic comeback - Leslie's feminist principles delivered with cutting wit

jokes.text: Now, if I wanna start my day off with a triple-decker pancake breakfast pizza, I gotta go to the one that's 10 minutes away, and that makes me 20 minutes late to work every day. I work at home.
jokes.setup: Complaint about park replacing fast food restaurant
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he works at home, making his complaint completely absurd
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds sympathy for commute problem then reveals it's self-created nonsense

jokes.text: Have you ever considered not eating that for breakfast? / I'll never consider that.
jokes.setup: Leslie's reasonable suggestion about breakfast choice
jokes.punchline: Bjorn's absolute refusal to consider any alternative
jokes.explanation: Good character stubbornness - the finality of 'never consider' is funny

jokes.text: How many people am I talking to, Donna? One or 1 1/9? / Are you asking me if I'm pregnant?
jokes.setup: Andy's attempt at subtle pregnancy investigation
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous math pun and Donna seeing right through it
jokes.explanation: Clever math pun attempt at subtlety, Donna's directness cuts through the nonsense

jokes.text: Whew. This case just got interesting. Well, not just. It was pretty interesting to begin with. This case just remained interesting.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on his investigation
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-correction and awkward backtracking
jokes.explanation: Good Andy overthinking moment - his attempt to sound cool getting tangled up

jokes.text: This town is going to hell in a Gucci knock-off handbag, girl.
jokes.setup: Complaint about moral decline in Pawnee
jokes.punchline: The specific fashion-conscious twist on the common expression
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar phrase - the Gucci knockoff detail adds character

jokes.text: Oh, I run a pornographic DVD emporium.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks about Dennis's business after he praised her
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that it's pornography
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Leslie's relief at positive feedback immediately undercut

jokes.text: I'm Brandi Maxxxx... Star of such films as the incredible Burt Wonderbone, A Good Guy to Lay Hard, and Argo. I had a small part in Argo as well as the porn version Our Goo.
jokes.setup: Brandi introducing herself and her film career
jokes.punchline: The porn parody titles, especially 'Our Goo' for 'Argo'
jokes.explanation: Clever porn parody titles, the Argo/Our Goo wordplay is particularly good

jokes.text: I've seen the first 90 seconds. It's thrilling.
jokes.setup: Brandi mentioning her role in Argo
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward attempt to relate while revealing how little she watched
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie awkwardness - trying to be supportive while clearly uncomfortable

jokes.text: Someone-- I'm not saying Diddy, but I'm not not saying Diddy-- has offered to buy out Rent-A-Swag for a buttload of money.
jokes.setup: Tom announcing the buyout offer to investors
jokes.punchline: His continued obsession with it being Diddy despite being told otherwise
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Diddy fixation, the double negative construction is funny

jokes.text: I'm talking pedicures on pedicures on pedicures.
jokes.setup: Donna excited about treat-yourself potential from sale
jokes.punchline: The absurd escalation of already excessive pampering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna indulgence fantasy - the repetition emphasizes the excess

jokes.text: I actually think that you should get a 'Hello Kitty' tattoo. / No, with the store. / Oh. Uh, burn it for the insurance money.
jokes.setup: Tom asking April's advice about the business
jokes.punchline: April's completely unrelated suggestion followed by criminal advice
jokes.explanation: Peak April unhelpfulness - completely missing the point then suggesting arson

jokes.text: God, nobody cares, Gary. / Enough. / Thank you.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to give advice
jokes.punchline: Immediate shutdown and Jerry accepting it
jokes.explanation: Standard Jerry abuse moment - the quick acceptance makes it funnier

jokes.text: We always use protection... but I'm pretty positive she pokes holes in them.
jokes.setup: Tom considering if Mona-Lisa could be pregnant
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of relationship sabotage by his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Tom's powerlessness in toxic relationship revealed casually

jokes.text: It's called 'Womb There It Is.' / That's her brand.
jokes.setup: Andy showing the pregnancy test brand
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous pun name and Tom's matter-of-fact recognition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun on 'Whoomp! There It Is' - absurd but perfectly logical for Mona-Lisa

jokes.text: I'm gonna have a baby with Jean-Ralphio's sister? Oh, God. What have I done? Like, to humanity?
jokes.setup: Tom realizing implications of pregnancy
jokes.punchline: His existential horror expanding to concern for all mankind
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from personal to cosmic horror - Tom's dramatic perspective is funny

jokes.text: I'm the Nipple King. Kernston's Rubber Nipple Company elects a nipple king and a nipple queen every year to ride on the float.
jokes.setup: Chris needing to tell Leslie something important
jokes.punchline: The completely unexpected and absurd royal title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with Chris delivering this ridiculous information with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: I'm the Nipple King.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining his role in the parade
jokes.punchline: The completely unexpected and bizarre title
jokes.explanation: Absolutely perfect absurd moment - Chris's earnest delivery makes it even funnier

jokes.text: No fun for you!
jokes.setup: Leslie watching the founder's week parade
jokes.punchline: Anti-Leslie float chanting at her during what should be a celebration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Leslie's nightmare scenario of public humiliation

jokes.text: No fun for you! Maybe it's not you. Maybe it's Kate Upton.
jokes.setup: Anti-Leslie float in the parade
jokes.punchline: Chris's desperate attempt to make Leslie feel better with an impossible comparison
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup with Chris's well-meaning but absurd attempt at comfort.

jokes.text: Recall Knope! / Legalize weed!
jokes.setup: Anti-Leslie crowd chanting
jokes.punchline: Someone randomly shouting unrelated political slogan
jokes.explanation: Standard comedy trope of random protester with unrelated agenda

jokes.text: Yeah. It's mine. / Lemony snicket.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa admitting the pregnancy test is hers
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's bizarre exclamation using author name as curse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio absurdity - using children's author as profanity substitute

jokes.text: Psych! Ah, ha, ha. Look at your stupid face. You had a dumdum face.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa admitting pregnancy then immediately
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a cruel joke with childish mockery
jokes.explanation: Peak Mona-Lisa cruelty - the 'dumdum face' insult is perfectly childish

jokes.text: Do you know how many different pills I take, birth control or otherwise? There is no way that I could be pregnant with a human baby.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa explaining why she can't be pregnant
jokes.punchline: The disturbing implication about her pill consumption and 'human baby' specification
jokes.explanation: Disturbing character detail - the 'human baby' qualifier suggests something very wrong

jokes.text: She's the worst. You dodged a bullet, baby. Congratulations. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio asking what's wrong with Tom being happy
jokes.punchline: Immediately agreeing his sister is terrible and congratulating Tom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jean-Ralphio's complete honesty about his sister being awful.

jokes.text: Besides, the math doesn't add up because we only have sex every single day.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining why April can't be pregnant
jokes.punchline: His logic that daily sex would prevent pregnancy somehow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic failure - completely misunderstanding how pregnancy works

jokes.text: When we went to Hawaii, you woke me up at 3:00 A.M. to watch Meet the Press. / Well, it was Elizabeth Warren. Like, I'm not going to watch that live?
jokes.setup: Ben pointing out Leslie's workaholic tendencies even on vacation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's complete justification as if it's obviously reasonable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her political obsession trumping all normal behavior

jokes.text: Step up. Step Up 2: The Streets. I'm sorry, my adrenaline is pumping, so all I can think of is dance movie titles. Stomp the Yard. There's one.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving defiant speech about recall
jokes.punchline: Her brain defaulting to dance movie titles under stress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character quirk - Leslie's mind going to random pop culture under pressure

jokes.text: You Got Served. Silver Linings Playbook, kind of.
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing with dance movie theme
jokes.punchline: The uncertainty about whether Silver Linings Playbook counts
jokes.explanation: Good callback continuation - the 'kind of' shows Leslie's analytical mind even in crisis

jokes.text: I think I'm better off. I haven't had a date in a while, but I'm learning a lot about me. / Well, I wasn't asking you specifically, but I'm happy to hear that, Shauna.
jokes.setup: Leslie's rhetorical question to the press
jokes.punchline: Reporter taking it personally and oversharing, Leslie's awkward response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - reporter's overshare catches Leslie off guard

jokes.text: Recall, shmecall. No, I can't end my big press conference by saying 'Recall, shmecall.' That's ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to end her press conference with authority
jokes.punchline: Her immediate self-awareness about how ridiculous her line sounds
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Leslie's self-awareness about her own rhetoric failing

jokes.text: That's another dance movie with Jessica Alba.
jokes.setup: Ben helping Leslie end the press conference
jokes.punchline: Leslie still stuck in dance movie mode
jokes.explanation: Weak continuation of callback - feels forced rather than organic

jokes.text: Sex is ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how things are going with Chris
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good Ann deadpan moment - the economy of the line makes it work

jokes.text: My wife is pregnant!
jokes.setup: Andy excited about pregnancy investigation
jokes.punchline: His announcement of the wrong conclusion
jokes.explanation: Decent Andy confusion but fairly predictable mistake

jokes.text: My wife is pregnant! Okay, put me down.
jokes.setup: Andy celebrating what he thinks is April's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan correction of his inappropriate celebration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Andy's enthusiastic misunderstanding and April's practical response.

jokes.text: I wanna wait until we're 50 and then adopt a set of creepy adult twins from Romania.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about pregnancy plans
jokes.punchline: April's characteristically dark and specific alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment - perfectly specific and disturbing, quintessentially her character

jokes.text: He's calling it 'Tommy's Closet.' It's a high-end secondhand clothing store where parents can rent clothes for their teens, tweens, and pres, as well as babies, toddlers, grown-ups, and olds.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explaining competitor's business model
jokes.punchline: The blatant copying of Tom's exact idea and demographic categories
jokes.explanation: Decent business comedy but feels a bit drawn out, the copying is obvious

jokes.text: So I guess only Domino's is allowed to sell pizza now? It's called capitalism, Mr. Haverford, and you are about to get a free lesson. Only this free lesson is going to cost you. So it's not free?
jokes.setup: Lawyer's dramatic business threat
jokes.punchline: His metaphor immediately falling apart and Tom pointing it out
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment - Tom's literal-minded response undercuts the threat

jokes.text: So I guess only Domino's is allowed to sell pizza now? It's called capitalism, Mr. Haverford, and you are about to get a free lesson. Only this free lesson is going to cost you.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about business copying
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of a 'free lesson' that 'will cost you'
jokes.explanation: The self-contradictory 'free lesson that will cost you' is a nice verbal contradiction

jokes.text: It is Diddy. / I knew it! / It's not Diddy.
jokes.setup: Tom still obsessed with Diddy connection
jokes.punchline: The lawyer's momentary confirmation then immediate correction
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to running gag - brief moment of vindication before deflation

jokes.text: It is Diddy. I knew it! It's not Diddy.
jokes.setup: Tom's continued Diddy obsession
jokes.punchline: The lawyer accidentally confirming then immediately denying
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running gag with the lawyer's slip showing Tom's obsession was right all along, then immediately wrong again.
